summary:
The use of an appropriate cement such as glass ionomers ensures adequate bond strength between the synthetic resin material and natural teeth; maintaining accurate occlusal relations remains critical regardless of which type of cement is used due to its impact on long-term function and patient satisfaction after provisional restoration placement. To ensure optimal results during this transitional phase, careful attention must also be paid to precise impressions when fabricating both proximal surfaces and opposing tooth structures before final fitting procedures commence later down the line once definitive restorations are ready. Given your instructions focusing only on essential aspects:

1) Appropriate cement choice supports structural integrity,
2) Precise replication of existing occlusions enhances functionality & comfort over time. These two main points encapsulate Agent’s primary concerns regarding treatment planning involving synthetic resins bridges temporally placed within oral cavities awaiting permanent solutions installation at some future date point determined clinically based upon individual case specifics including but certainly not limited solely too aesthetic considerations alone!
